description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular shape and a woodgrain-patterned exterior on the top and sides. The front features a large, rounded rectangular black screen, flanked by a set of rotary dials and buttons on the right for channel and volume control. Two thin metal antenna rods extend upward from a black base on the top surface, giving it a classic retro appearance. The back of the television is a protruding black plastic housing, likely for the cathode ray tube and internal electronics, with visible ventilation slats and a power cord extending outward. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-to-late 20th-century home televisions, with a sturdy build and tactile controls. The object combines wood, plastic, and metal elements, making it both functional and decorative in an urban or domestic setting.
description: A vintage box-shaped television with a woodgrain exterior, black screen, rotary dials, and metal antennae, featuring a protruding back and power cord.
description_view_0: Side view showing the woodgrain panel, antenna base, and the protruding black back housing with the power cord.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the black screen, rotary dials, buttons, and the woodgrain top with antennae.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with woodgrain panel, antenna base, and the black back housing visible.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the black plastic back, ventilation slats, and the power cord extending outward.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel with rounded corners and a matte finish, no visible branding or decorative elements.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and buttons on the front right panel.
